videoTitle: Reliance Q4 FY26: Record Profit, Jio Hits 200M 5G Users
targetAudience: Business professionals and retail investors tracking Reliance Industries
openingHook: India biggest company just posted its highest-ever quarterly profit — and the number will surprise you.

segments.narrationScript: India biggest company just posted its highest-ever quarterly profit of Rs21,243 crore. That is more than the GDP of some Indian states — in just three months.
segments.visualTreatment: Full-screen animated counter ticking up to Rs21,243 Cr against dark background with Reliance logo
segments.dataOverlay: Rs21,243 Cr | Q4 FY26 Net Profit
segments.bRoll: Reliance Industries headquarters in Mumbai, stock ticker showing RIL

segments.narrationScript: Reliance Industries — the company behind Jio, Reliance Retail, and one of Asia largest oil refineries — reported revenue of Rs2.6 lakh crore for the quarter. That is a 14% jump from last year. The star performer? Jio, which crossed 200 million 5G subscribers — a milestone no telecom company in the world has hit this fast.
segments.visualTreatment: Split screen: Jio 5G tower on left, Reliance Retail store on right. Animated revenue bar chart building up
segments.dataOverlay: Revenue: Rs2.6 Lakh Cr | Jio 5G Users: 200M
segments.bRoll: Jio 5G rollout footage, Reliance Retail stores, refinery operations

segments.narrationScript: Let us break down where the money came from. Oil-to-chemicals contributed 42% of EBITDA. Jio added 28%. Retail brought in 22%. And the new green energy business, still in early stages, contributed 8%. The key takeaway: Reliance is no longer just an oil company. Digital and retail now drive half the profits.
segments.visualTreatment: Animated pie chart building segment by segment with color coding for each business
segments.bRoll: Oil refinery, Jio offices, retail stores, solar panels

segments.narrationScript: What does this mean for investors? Reliance stock has returned 18% in the last 12 months, outperforming the Nifty 50 by 6 percentage points. Analysts at Morgan Stanley have a target price of Rs3,200 — 15% upside from current levels. The big catalyst to watch: the Jio IPO, which Mukesh Ambani has hinted could happen in FY28. When Jio lists separately, it could be valued at Rs8-10 lakh crore on its own.
segments.visualTreatment: Stock price chart with 12-month performance vs Nifty. Analyst target price displayed prominently
segments.dataOverlay: RIL 12M Return: +18% | Analyst Target: Rs3,200 | Jio Standalone Value: Rs8-10 Lakh Cr
segments.bRoll: BSE trading floor, analyst reports, Mukesh Ambani at AGM

segments.narrationScript: The bottom line: Reliance is firing on all cylinders. Record profit, record 5G subscribers, and a potential Jio IPO on the horizon. For the full analysis including what this means for your portfolio, head to ET Markets. I am Priya Sharma, and this is ET Now.
segments.visualTreatment: ET Now logo animation, anchor sign-off with lower third. QR code to ET Markets article
